author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

title: Chapter 13

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: Over and over again He reproaches the false speakers and liars in such terms as these: ‘Ye are the children of the devil, for he also was a slanderer from the beginning, and abode not in the truth.’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: We are not wont to say, "He was a slanderer," but "He was a murderer."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But we ask how it was that the devil was a murderer from the beginning; and we find that he slew the first man, not by drawing a sword, nor by applying to him any bodily violence, but by persuading him to sin, and thus driving him from the happiness of Paradise.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What, then, was Paradise is now represented by the Church.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Therefore those are the sons of the devil who slay men by withdrawing them from the Church.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But as by the words of God we know what was the situation of Paradise, so now by the words of Christ we have learned where the Church is to be found: "Throughout all nations," He says, "beginning at Jerusalem."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Whosoever, therefore, separates a man from that complete whole to place him in any single part, is proved to be a son of the devil and a murderer.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But see, further, what is the application of the expression which you yourself employed in saying of the devil, "He was a slanderer, and abode not in the truth."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For you bring an accusation against the whole world on account of the sins of others, though even those others themselves you were more able to accuse than to convict; and you abode not in the truth of Christ.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For He says that the Church is "throughout all nations, beginning at Jerusalem;" but ye say that it is in the party of Donatus.
